messages.content:
Summarize how UNIX systems are affected by this vulnerability in Go.

Go before 1.16.12 and 1.17.x before 1.17.5 on UNIX allows write operations to an unintended file or unintended network connection as a consequence of erroneous closing of file descriptor 0 after file-descriptor exhaustion.

On UNIX systems, the improper management of file descriptors in certain versions of Go can lead to unintentional file or network writes due to the erroneous closure of file descriptor 0 following exhaustion of available file descriptors, potentially causing data mishandling.
